apex_basket.value_chain_position: Financial-management platform layer — QuickBooks is the ~85%-share SMB accounting/payroll OS, TurboTax dominates assisted tax, Credit Karma is the consumer financial-data network; the operating layer for small-business finance.
apex_basket.thesis: The deepest-margin-of-safety guard-clean compounder in the universe and the book's anchor — an 85%-share SMB-finance OS with an expanding-margin moat trading at +64% to base SoP, a credible +30% over 12 months with multi-year re-rate optionality.
apex_basket.theme_durability: fintech_rails toll on virtually every US SMB; QuickBooks Online international + Credit Karma monetization keep secular demand growing 14-15% with no single-customer dependence.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: company-wide gross margin ~78%->80.8% (FY23->FY25) on +14% 3yr CAGR, non-GAAP operating margin +450bps to 58% — pricing power, not commoditization (watch-item: TurboTax-DIY 'lost on price' to free/AI, masked by TurboTax-Live mix +36%).
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: ROIC ~12% and rising, $7.7B TTM FCF (37% FCF margin) redeployed into Credit Karma/AI; rule-of-40 = 51.9 with capex-light SaaS economics.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +64% / ev_gp 4.88 vs +15% rev — guard passes clean (cheapest gross-profit multiple among the apex), no deduction.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — diversified Fortune-500-to-SMB base, 85% QuickBooks share, debate verdict B / value_conviction 5.

wheel.rationale: Get paid to wait at the +64% MoS: sell the put at the downside-to-break, cover-call the re-rate at base fair value.

apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = continued double-digit rev compounding + Credit Karma/AI monetization re-rating a name that trades at +64% to base SoP; the full +70% to base is a 12-18mo story but +30-35% is credible inside a year off the deepest discount in the book.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Advanced-fab layer — the world's sole source of leading-edge logic (3nm/2nm) that every AI accelerator, custom ASIC and smartphone AP must be built on; ~100% of sub-5nm capacity.
apex_basket.thesis: The supply-chain chokepoint of the entire AI build-out, raised toward full size under the accelerating-growth read because N2-sold-out + rising scarcity rent give it the cleanest live earnings-momentum path to +30% of the AI-capex legs; combined-capped with NVDA.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure — revenue +31% YoY, N2 sold out, sole-source leading edge; Intel 18A years behind, Samsung trails on yield; the most structural moat in the book.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin 54.4%->59.9%->66.2% (FY23->Q1'26) on +36% USD revenue — rising scarcity rent / pricing power, only deliberate overseas-fab + N2-ramp dilution as offsets.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 27% FCF margin on a heavy-capex model but capex efficiency improving as leading-edge ASPs rise; ~NT$1.1T TTM FCF, net cash; rule-of-40 = 57.8.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$1,180/ADR sits ABOVE the ~$424 ADR quote (the SoP capitalizes local EPS x ADR ratio); guard passes — size is held by the AI-capex combined cap, not by the guard.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, conviction 4; customer-concentration + Taiwan geopolitical single-point-of-failure are the structural tails behind the combined cap.
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = the live earnings inflection — gross margin running 59.9%->66.2% on +31% revenue with N2 fully booked; the ADR has compounded with EPS and another +25-35% inside a year is consistent with the GM/volume ramp, the highest-confidence near-term mover of the AI-capex trio.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Vertically integrated uranium miner/processor — Tier-1 assets (Cigar Lake, McArthur River) plus conversion and the 49% Westinghouse stake; the nuclear-fuel chokepoint for the existing fleet and the SMR build-out.
apex_basket.thesis: The highest-quality public uranium toll-taker and the book's cleanest orthogonal reach — a live contract-re-pricing + AI-baseload-demand re-rate that does NOT ride the hyperscaler capex line, with bull SoP ~$133-143 = +32-42% inside the horizon.
apex_basket.theme_durability: energy_transition / nuclear-fuel supply — long-term contract prices re-pricing to the highest since 2008 (~$90/lb), AI-baseload demand + signed ~$80B US-gov Westinghouse program; load_bearing 5, interrogator 5.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector AMBER: consolidated gross margin COMPRESSED 33.9%->26.7% (FY24->FY25) on cost inflation/Inkai sourcing even as realized contract prices rose — pricing power is in contract prices, not yet in consolidated GM, which must re-expand as higher-priced contracts roll on.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 25% FCF margin, ~C$1.9B FY25 adj-EBITDA, net-near-flat leverage (-0.1x); incremental returns ride re-pricing of the long-term contract book plus Westinghouse distributions; rule-of-40 = 32.9.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$95-100 brackets the ~$101 quote (~flat); guard passes but the amber GM keeps size disciplined.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, contract-backlog re-pricing; commodity/uranium-spot and GM-compression are the watch-items.
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = higher-priced long-term contracts rolling onto the book re-expanding consolidated GM, plus uranium-spot momentum on AI-baseload demand; bull SoP +32-42% is a live trend, not 'eventually' — the orthogonal reach that lets the book chase the goal without adding AI-capex beta.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Spine-surgery implants + the ExcelsiusGPS robotic-navigation platform — capital equipment drives per-procedure implant consumable pull-through; the largest pure-play spine platform post-NuVasive.
apex_basket.thesis: A guard-clean robotics leg with an expanding-margin implant annuity and a live placement-driven pull-through inflection — bull SoP $132 = +39% — diversifying the book into surgical robotics orthogonal to the AI-capex line.
apex_basket.theme_durability: robotics_automation / surgical robotics — ExcelsiusGPS taking share from Medtronic Mazor and Stryker Mako-spine; the robot is the razor that pulls through high-margin implants; load_bearing 4.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin ~67.4% rising for six straight quarters, base-business adj-EBITDA back to mid-30s (35.7% Q4'25) on +16.7% revenue — pricing power, not commoditization.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: ROIC ~6.2% (depressed by NuVasive integration, normalizing), net cash (-0.5x), $605M TTM FCF; incremental returns ride robot placements -> implant pull-through; rule-of-40 = 43.0.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +12% / ev_gp 5.11x vs +23% rev — guard passes clean (one of the cheapest gross-profit multiples in the book).
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, interrogator 4; watch robot-placement competition (Medtronic Stealth AXiS / Stryker Mako-spine) and the 6.2% ROIC normalization.

wheel.rationale: Own it lower at the thesis break, cap the re-rate at base fair value while the integration-suppressed ROIC normalizes.

apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = NuVasive-integration margin normalization (six straight quarters of GM expansion) plus ExcelsiusGPS placements pulling through high-margin implants; bull SoP +39% off a still-positive +12% base MoS is a credible within-year earnings-inflection path.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: AI-accelerator silicon + the CUDA software platform — the dominant compute layer every LLM training/inference workload must run on; ~80-90% of AI-training revenue.
apex_basket.thesis: The most load-bearing chokepoint in the universe and the compute leg of the AI-capex axis, raised to full size under the accelerating-growth read because the Blackwell ramp gives a live +38% bull path; combined-capped with TSM because they share the hyperscaler-capex driver.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure — DC revenue +142% YoY, top-5 hyperscalers ~50%+ of DC revenue (the concentration tell); secular but the demand is one capex line that can digest.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: non-GAAP gross margin rose to ~75% (Q1'27) WHILE revenue grew +114% then +65% — durable pricing power into the largest ramp in tech history.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Elite ~45% ROIC on incremental capital, 47% FCF margin, $119B TTM FCF, net cash; capex-light fabless model redeploys at extreme returns; rule-of-40 = 117.6.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +2.3% — base SoP ~$210 sits on top of the ~$205 price (no discount); bull SoP ~$283 = +38%; peak_flag true (the air-pocket case) — guard does not veto but argues for the combined-capped size.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high but cycle-sensitive — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5; a 2-quarter hyperscaler digestion pause is the central risk and the reason for the combined cap.
apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = the Blackwell/GB-series ramp carrying DC revenue and a rising ~75% GM; bull SoP +38% is a live-momentum path within the year. Held at the AI-capex combined cap (not above it) because the same hyperscaler-capex digestion that powers the +38% is also the -34% air-pocket risk.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Diversified medtech — cardiovascular, neuromodulation, urology and endoscopy devices including pulsed-field-ablation (Farawave) and the Ion robotic-bronchoscopy platform; per-procedure consumable pull-through on a robotics_automation razor/blade.
apex_basket.thesis: The deepest guard pass in the universe promoted into the apex under the goal — bear SoP ~$62 sits ABOVE the ~$47 price, i.e. a downside-PROTECTED reach with sop_mos +66% — a PFA/electrophysiology + robotic-bronchoscopy grower whose only blemish is a first-guided GM step-down (amber, not red).
apex_basket.theme_durability: robotics_automation / structural-heart + EP — Farawave PFA is the fastest-growing segment in cardiac ablation and a share-gainer vs Medtronic/J&J; Ion robotic bronchoscopy adds a second robotics leg; load_bearing 4, +17% YoY at $70B+ scale.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector AMBER (the only blemish): gross margin ~69% FY25 with adj op-margin walked to 28%, BUT management GUIDED FY26 gross margin 'slightly lower' on adverse high-margin EP/WATCHMAN mix — first guided compression after years of expansion = moat amber as PFA competition enters; net margin still rising and FCF strong.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: ROIC building, $3.44B TTM FCF (16.7% FCF margin), net-funded leverage 2.0x with 11.6x coverage; incremental returns ride PFA capital placements -> consumable pull-through; rule-of-40 = 34.1.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +66.3% / ev_gp 5.84x — guard passes DEEP; the adverse-case SoP ~$62 is +32% ABOVE the current ~$47 price, so the name is structurally downside-cushioned even in a 12-18mo theme pause.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — interrogator 4, but trajectory PIVOTING and the FY26 GM guide-down is the watch-item; the deep guard cushion is what earns the seat despite the amber.

wheel.rationale: Deep guard + bear-FV-above-price makes this an ideal wheel: get paid below the thesis break, cap at the +66% base fair value while the amber GM guide plays out.

apex_basket.goal_note: 12-mo driver = Farawave PFA share gains compounding revenue + a base SoP $78 that is +66% above spot; this is the rare goal-fit name where the upside (+40-66%) and the downside protection (bear FV above price) BOTH hold — a downside-cushioned reach, sized at 0.9 only because of the amber FY26 GM guide.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Global payment-network operator — VisaNet connects ~15,000 issuers to ~100M merchant locations, taking a per-transaction toll on every card-based payment globally; the canonical fintech-rails chokepoint.
apex_basket.thesis: A load-bearing duopoly rail with retained pricing power and a VAS software re-rate optionality, sized DOWN to ballast under the goal because its 12-mo upside (+20% bull / at-spot base) is the smallest of the slate — orthogonal rate-duration anchor, not a reach.
apex_basket.theme_durability: fintech_rails — duopoly network effect no entrant has broken at scale; net revenue accelerated +9%->+17% across FY2Q25-FY2Q26, ~30% VAS software mix compounding.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: ~65-67% GAAP operating margin retained while net revenue accelerated and EPS +35% latest-Q — pricing power retained even as lower-margin VAS/Visa-Direct mixed in.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 49% FCF margin, $21.2B TTM FCF; VAS (~30% of revenue, software-grade margins) is the incremental-return engine; rule-of-40 = 63.6.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$300-305 sits just below the ~$320 quote (~-5%); guard passes with no size penalty — the small size reflects the goal (modest 12-mo upside), not a guard deduction.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; the structural watch-item is agentic/stablecoin rail-bypass, an overhang not yet evidence.
apex_basket.goal_note: Does NOT clear the +30-50% bar in 12 months (bull SoP ~$385 = +20%, base at-spot) — kept as the lowest-volatility rate-duration ballast that anchors the book's downside in a NEUTRAL/late-cycle regime; a quality compounder earning a smaller seat, not a reach.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Veterinary-diagnostics toll-taker — in-clinic analyzers + recurring consumable test kits + a direct reference-lab network; a razor/blade installed base that is deeply sticky once placed.
apex_basket.thesis: An orthogonal, guard-clean compounder with 36% ROIC and an expanding-margin razor/blade moat — sized DOWN to ballast under the goal because its 12-mo upside (+18% bull / +9.6% base) is modest, but it remains the book's purest diversifier away from every shared axis.
apex_basket.theme_durability: genomics_bio_tools / companion-animal diagnostics — secular pet-health spend, no scaled public competitor, durable consumable annuity per placed instrument.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: comparable gross margin 61.8%->63.4% (FY25->Q1'26, +90bps) on +10-12% organic revenue — pricing power, not commoditization.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Elite 36% ROIC (return on incremental capital), +10-12% organic with double-digit international runway, rule-of-40 = 37.4 and 24% FCF margin.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +9.6% / ev_gp 16.9x vs +13% rev — guard passes (price ~$561 sits below base SoP ~$615); no deduction.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; no AI-capex, China, or single-customer exposure — the most orthogonal name in the book.

wheel.rationale: A slow at-fair-value compounder you would happily own lower: sell the put at the thesis break, cover-call the modest re-rate at base fair value.

apex_basket.goal_note: Does NOT clear +30-50% in 12 months (bull SoP $685 = +18%, base +9.6%) — earns a smaller seat as the single fully-orthogonal (zero shared-axis) 36%-ROIC compounder, the diversification ballast the high-beta AI-capex reaches require; a longer-horizon hold, not a 12-mo mover.

runner_ups.thesis: The power-and-cooling leg of the AI-capex axis and the most guard-clean of the three AI-infra chokepoints — DROPPED from the apex to the bench this run as the diversify-swap that resolves the AI-capex cap (lowest 12-mo upside of the trio: bull +12% / consensus +24%). First back in if an AI-capex slot frees and the cap allows.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos -7.6% — price ~$303 brackets the base SoP ~$300; guard passes, but its membership in the AI-capex axis (with NVDA+TSM) is what bumped it to the bench.

runner_ups.thesis: Cheap (ev_gp 6.8), guard-clean (sop_mos +15.7), expanding ~84%-GM industrial-software/PLM toll with a goal-fit bull path (+45-58%); held off the apex ONLY by the 3-per-Technology GICS cap behind INTU/TSM/NVDA — the first Tech name in if a Tech slot frees.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +15.7% / ev_gp 6.77x vs +28% rev — guard passes clean.

runner_ups.thesis: Guard-clean enterprise-workflow + AI-agent orchestration toll (GM flat-high 77.5%, op-margin expanding, sop_mos +15.5); blocked by the 3-per-Technology GICS cap. Highest-scoring bench name but its 12-mo upside (~+15% base) is below the goal bar.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +15.5% / ev_gp 9.9x vs +22% rev — guard passes clean.

runner_ups.thesis: Guard-clean (sop_mos +24.9), goal-fit restaurant-vertical payment OS — GM expanding, net income inflected positive, bull SoP $42 = +73% — blocked by the Technology GICS cap behind INTU/TSM/NVDA; the highest-upside Tech bench name.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +24.9% / ev_gp 7.56x vs +23% rev — guard passes clean (bear SoP $22 net-cash-floored, ~-10%).

runner_ups.thesis: 91%-GM AEC/manufacturing CAD workflow monopoly, deepest margin-of-safety in Tech (sop_mos +32%, bear-SoP ~$212 ABOVE the ~$198 price = downside-protected, bull +36%); bench behind the Technology GICS cap.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +32.3% / ev_gp 6.95x vs +18% rev — guard passes clean (bear-SoP sits above the price).

macro_read: macro_regime.json reads NEUTRAL (score 0.567) with growth ACCELERATING but inflation ACCELERATING and credit COMPLACENT (late-cycle) and rates neutral — so against the +30-50%/12-mo goal I take a balanced-leaning-REACH stance: lean into the live earnings-momentum AI-capex chokepoints (NVDA/TSM raised toward full size) and the uranium/medtech re-rates that can deliver the goal within a year, while sizing the high-beta reaches with discipline and keeping V/IDXX as rate-duration ballast because accelerating inflation + complacent credit cap how aggressive a NEUTRAL regime should be.

disruptor_memo.rubric_and_weighting: Four pillars ~25 pts each applied ONLY to names clearing the gates: (1) theme durability & load-bearing position, (2) moat & pricing power with the gross-margin trajectory as the lie-detector, (3) reinvestment runway & incremental ROIC, (4) growth-adjusted valuation as a GUARD ONLY. Pillars 1-3 set the ranking; valuation (sop_mos_pct, ev_gp, rule_of_40, peak_flag) acted purely as a guard that could deduct, cap, or veto, and was NEVER the reason one name ranked above another that passed the guard clean. This is a GOAL-DRIVEN RE-PICK over the same 40 debated names (no new debates): the +30-50%/~12-month return goal and the macro read re-weighted SIZING and membership toward names with a live 12-mo re-rate driver, within the unchanged gates and guard.
disruptor_memo.gate_results: All 40 debated names cleared the hard gates as pre-stamped and re-verified: every name is ttm_fcf_positive=true, rev_growth_gate=true, funded_solvency != weak (all strong/moderate/exempt_financial), and forensic_gate is empty (no EXCLUDE, no CAP). No name was rendered ineligible by a gate, so differentiation came from the rubric, the valuation guard, and the 12-mo goal overlay.
disruptor_memo.excluded_by_forensic_gate: None — no name carried forensic_gate=EXCLUDE or CAP.
disruptor_memo.excluded_by_hard_gates: None on FCF/growth/solvency — all 40 passed. (BLLN, BE, IOT, NVT, TOST etc. are FCF-positive on a TTM basis; no fcf_inflecting-only name had to be carried, so the <=0.5 inflecting rule did not bind.)
disruptor_memo.deducted_or_vetoed_by_valuation_guard: Guard VETO / size-0.5 territory (sop_mos_pct <= -40%, the CRO cannot get near the price) removed: ASML (-99.9%), BE (-81.7%), PLTR (-79.3%), HWM (-72.2%), ARM (-54.0%), WWD (-53.5%). Guard DEDUCTED (price above base SoP, -20% to -30%) and kept off the apex: ANET (-26.5), CRDO (-22.3), APP (-21.5), FICO (-21.1, also verdict C/conviction 2), GE (-28.4, C), ISRG (-29.5, C), HEI (-20.1), MEDP (-20.3, C + rising customer concentration into cancelling metabolic names), WST (-22.4, C). Under the GOAL these guard-failures hurt twice: the names with the most aggressive embedded S-curves (ARM, PLTR, CRDO, ASML) are exactly the ones where a +30-50% 12-mo move would require multiple EXPANSION from an already-stretched base — the goal does not rescue a guard veto.
disruptor_memo.fcf_inflecting_keeps: None. No apex pick is fcf_inflecting; every one is ttm_fcf_positive=true on a trailing basis, so no name required the guidance/backlog FCF-turn evidence or the <=0.5 size cap on that basis.
disruptor_memo.rise_fall_vs_prior_apex: Prior disruptor apex (caller-specified) = [INTU, IDXX, V, CCJ, GMED, NVDA, TSM, VRT]. New apex = [INTU, TSM, CCJ, GMED, NVDA, BSX, V, IDXX]. DROPPED: VRT -> bench (the diversify-swap that resolves the AI-capex cap; lowest 12-mo upside of the three AI-capex legs at bull +12% / consensus +24%, so it is the natural leg to cut when only two AI-capex names fit under the 30% ceiling at goal-sized weights). ADDED: BSX -> apex (the goal promotes the deepest guard pass in the universe: sop_mos +66%, bear FV ~$62 ABOVE the ~$47 price = a downside-PROTECTED reach with +40-66% upside; the FY26 GM-guide amber holds its size to 0.9 and the prior run had it benched). SIZE RISES driven by the goal: TSM 0.80 -> 1.05 and NVDA 0.85 -> 1.00 (full-size the two live-earnings-momentum AI-capex chokepoints under accelerating growth), CCJ 1.05 -> 1.05 held (live uranium re-rate, orthogonal), GMED 0.95 -> 1.00 (live placement->implant inflection). SIZE FALLS driven by the goal: INTU 1.20 -> 1.15 (still the anchor, trimmed only to fund the reaches), IDXX 1.15 -> 0.75 and V 1.05 -> 0.80 (the two lowest 12-mo-upside names, meets_goal=false, demoted to rate-duration ballast). Net unit total 7.70 -> 7.70.

theme_concentration_stress.ai-capex (MANDATORY check): CARRIES 2 names after the swap — NVDA (compute) and TSM (foundry/supply-chain) — the two legs of the SAME hyperscaler-capex line; a 2-quarter digestion pause hits both at once and they measured 0.64 correlated (2y weekly). The PRIOR run carried 3 (NVDA/TSM/VRT, the 0.64-0.65 cluster) and capped them at 2.30 units / 29.9%. THIS run, to goal-size NVDA+TSM toward full units, I DIVERSIFIED rather than cap-squeeze: dropped VRT (the lowest-upside, +12% base leg) to the bench, leaving NVDA+TSM combined-capped at {names:[NVDA,TSM], max_units:2.05, axis:'ai-capex'} = 26.6% of the 7.70-unit book, under the 30% ceiling. Argued against the real numbers: NVDA-TSM corr 0.64 is the binding pair; at 26.6% combined a single hyperscaler-capex cut impairs <27% of the book, vs the 3-leg cluster (NVDA/TSM/VRT all 0.64-0.65 pairwise) which would have concentrated the same systematic shock across three positions. The ai_infrastructure THEME id independently caps at 2 names / 26.6% — both constraints satisfied.
theme_concentration_stress.supply-chain-tsmc: CARRIES 2 names — TSM (the foundry itself) and NVDA (fabbed at TSMC); a Taiwan/advanced-node disruption hits both. RESOLVED = bounded by the same ai-capex combined cap (both members), within the 2.05-unit / 26.6% ceiling.
theme_concentration_stress.china-export-controls: CARRIES 2 names — NVDA and TSM (both with China revenue + license/geopolitical exposure). RESOLVED = covered by the same ai-capex combined cap (both members), so a single export-control ruling is bounded by the 2.05-unit / 26.6% ceiling; no separate swap required.
theme_concentration_stress.single-customer-hyperscaler: CARRIES 2 names — NVDA and TSM ultimately depend on the same handful of hyperscaler buyers (VRT, the third dependent, was diversified out to the bench). RESOLVED = identical membership to the ai-capex cap, so the 2.05-unit / 26.6% ceiling already constrains it.
theme_concentration_stress.rate-duration: CARRIES 3 names — INTU, V, and IDXX (long-duration growth/quality multiples that compress together when real rates rise). RESOLVED = kept WITHOUT a numeric combined cap because they sit across DIFFERENT GICS sectors (Technology / Financial Services / Healthcare) and orthogonal sub-drivers (SMB-finance software / payment network / vet-diagnostics annuity), all are guard-clean, and two of the three (V, IDXX) are deliberately ballast-sized at 0.80/0.75. A rate shock is a multiple event, not a thesis break, for all three; the trio sums to 35.0% but spans three sectors and three themes so no single theme/sector cap is breached, and in a NEUTRAL/neutral-rates regime this is the intended defensive ballast against the AI-capex beta.
theme_concentration_stress.medtech-capital-cycle: CARRIES 2 names — GMED and BSX (both ride hospital/surgical capital-equipment placement -> consumable pull-through). RESOLVED = kept WITHOUT a numeric cap: they are different sub-verticals (spine robotics vs cardiac PFA/EP), sum to 24.7% under the 30% robotics_automation theme cap which binds first, and BSX is the deepest-guarded name in the book (bear FV above price) so the pair's downside is structurally cushioned rather than correlated to a thesis break.
theme_concentration_stress.energy_transition / nuclear-fuel: CARRIES 1 name in the apex — CCJ only (VRT, which carried the energy_transition 2nd theme, was moved to the bench). No >=2-name energy axis remains; CCJ's nuclear-fuel-cycle + commodity-spot exposure is idiosyncratic and orthogonal to every other axis in the book.

disruptor_memo.construction_note: GOAL-DRIVEN RE-PICK: under a balanced-leaning-REACH stance (NEUTRAL regime, accelerating growth), sizing was tilted toward names with a LIVE 12-month re-rate driver — TSM/NVDA full-sized on earnings momentum, CCJ on uranium contract re-pricing, GMED on placement->implant inflection, BSX promoted as a downside-protected reach (bear FV above price). The two lowest-12-mo-upside compounders (V, IDXX, both meets_goal=false) were demoted to ballast, not dropped, because in a late-cycle NEUTRAL tape with accelerating inflation the book still needs rate-duration anchors. The AI-capex 30% ceiling was honored by DIVERSIFYING (VRT -> bench) rather than cap-squeezing three legs, which let NVDA+TSM run at goal size while keeping the binding 0.64 NVDA-TSM correlation bounded at 26.6% of the book. Six of eight apex names clear the +30-50%/12-mo goal (INTU, TSM, CCJ, GMED, NVDA, BSX); V and IDXX are the deliberate sub-goal ballast. Valuation acted only as a guard throughout — the richest hypergrowth disruptors (ARM, ASML, PLTR, APP, CRDO, HWM, WWD) were vetoed/deducted by the guard despite the strongest theme stories, and the goal did not override that because a +30-50% move on an already-stretched multiple would require expansion the guard rejects.

stress_test.note: Market-based stress: weighted basket return to the 52-week lows, and to 52w-lows -15% (recession). cro_bear is the agents' own adverse SoP (bear_fv_px, theme-pause case); when missing or implying upside it is flagged invalid and the published downside is the market-based recession stress.

correlation.fx_note: betas vs SMH (semis) + QQQ (long-duration tech) — the AI-capex/rate-duration systematic axes.
